content.text:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3389

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A19": 767, "B91": 533, "A30": 514, "B11": 9, "C47": 539, "C75": 125, "C26": 153, "C73": 325, "A47": 937, "A15": 745, "A75": 334, "A39": 804, "A56": 336, "C24": 587, "B26": 71}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A19": 138, "B91": 583, "A30": 868, "B11": 822, "C47": 783, "C75": 65, "C26": 262, "C73": 121, "A47": 508, "A15": 780, "A75": 461, "A39": 484, "A56": 668, "C24": 389, "B26": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this negotiation game:

1. I need to maximize my total importance while staying within the effort LIMIT of 3389
2. I don't know the other participant's importance values
3. I need to propose items that benefit me most

Let me calculate the efficiency (importance/effort) for each item:

A19: 138/767 = 0.180
B91: 583/533 = 1.094
A30: 868/514 = 1.689
B11: 822/9 = 91.333 (EXCELLENT!)
C47: 783/539 = 1.453
C75: 65/125 = 0.520
C26: 262/153 = 1.712
C73: 121/325 = 0.372
A47: 508/937 = 0.542
A15: 780/745 = 1.047
A75: 461/334 = 1.380
A39: 484/804 = 0.602
A56: 668/336 = 1.988
C24: 389/587 = 0.663
B26: 808/71 = 11.380 (EXCELLENT!)

Ranking by efficiency:
1. B11: 91.333
2. B26: 11.380
3. A56: 1.988
4. C26: 1.712
5. A30: 1.689
6. C47: 1.453
7. A75: 1.380
8. B91: 1.094
9. A15: 1.047
10. C24: 0.663
11. A39: 0.602
12. A47: 0.542
13. C75: 0.520
14. C73: 0.372
15. A19: 0.180

Let me try to build an optimal set starting with the most efficient items:


B11 (9) + B26 (71) + A56 (336) + C26 (153) + A30 (514) + C47 (539) + A75 (334) + B91 (533) + A15 (745) = 3234 effort, yielding 6035 importance. I have 155 effort remaining, so I should check if I can swap anything or add another item to improve this total.

Looking at what's left, only C75 fits within the remaining budget at 125 effort with 65 importance. Adding it brings the total to 3359 effort and 6100 importance—a solid proposal.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the proposal from the other player.

Proposed items: {'B11', 'B26', 'A56', 'C26', 'A30', 'C47', 'A75', 'B91', 'A15', 'C75'}

Let me calculate the total effort:
- B11: 9
- B26: 71
- A56: 336
- C26: 153
- A30: 514
- C47: 539
- A75: 334
- B91: 533
- A15: 745
- C75: 125

Total effort = 9 + 71 + 336 + 153 + 514 + 539 + 334 + 533 + 745 + 125 = 3359

This is within the limit of 3389.

Now let me calculate the importance to me:
- B11: 822
- B26: 808
- A56: 668
- C26: 262
- A30: 868
- C47: 783
- A75: 461
- B91: 583
- A15: 780
- C75: 65

Total importance to me = 822 + 808 + 668 + 262 + 868 + 783 + 461 + 583 + 780 + 65 = 6100

This is pretty good! But let me see if I can do better. The item C75 has very low importance to me (65) but uses 125 effort. Maybe I can swap it for something better.

Remaining effort if I remove C75: 3359 - 125 = 3234
Available effort: 3389 - 3234 = 155

Items not in the proposal:
- A19: effort 767, importance 138 (too high effort)
- A47: effort 937, importance 508 (too high effort)


- A39: effort 804, importance 484 (too high effort)
- C73: effort 325, importance 121 (too high effort)
- C24: effort 587, importance 389 (too high effort)

None of these fit within the remaining budget, so swapping C75 won't help. The proposal delivers strong value with multiple high-importance items, so I should accept it.
